headline: A $1 Million Portfolio That Quietly Pays You $67,500 a Year, No Job Required
summary: A $1 million portfolio generating $67,500 a year requires a blended yield of 6.75%. That sits at the crossover between moderate and aggressive income tiers, involving real tradeoffs most income calculators never explain. Why a 3.5% Yield Pays Less Than a Treasury Bond Right Now At 3.5% yield, $67,500 annually requires approximately $1,929,000 in invested ... A $1 Million Portfolio That Quietly Pays You $67,500 a Year, No Job Required

headline: How Much You Really Need Invested to Replace a $100,000 Salary With Dividends
summary: A $2 million portfolio generating $100,000 a year in dividends sounds like a retirement finish line. But the math depends entirely on which yield you accept, and that choice determines how much risk you carry for the rest of your life. The anchor equation is simple: divide your income target by the yield, and you ... How Much You Really Need Invested to Replace a $100,000 Salary With Dividends
